items.title: Axial Stress and Strain — quick review
items.body:
**Normal stress:** $\sigma = P/A$ (uniform, centroidal load only)

**Axial deformation:** $\delta = PL/(AE)$, equivalently $\delta = \varepsilon L$ where $\varepsilon = \sigma/E$ (Hooke's Law)

**Multi-segment members:** $\delta_{total} = \sum P_iL_i/(A_iE_i)$ — sum with sign (tension positive, compression negative)

**Statically indeterminate axial systems:** equilibrium alone is insufficient; add a compatibility (deformation) equation.

**Common units:** N, mm, MPa (N/mm²) is the most convenient consistent set for board problems.

items.title: Torsion — quick review
items.body:
**Polar moment of inertia (solid circular shaft):** $J = \pi d^4/32$

**Shear stress:** $\tau = Tr/J$, maximum at the outer fiber: $\tau_{max} = 16T/(\pi d^3)$

**Angle of twist:** $\phi = TL/(JG)$ — result is in **radians**; convert to degrees by multiplying by $180/\pi$

**Power-torque relationship:** $T = 9549\,P_{kW}/N_{rpm}$ (N·m)

**Hollow shaft:** $J = \pi(d_o^4 - d_i^4)/32$

items.title: Flexural Stress — quick review
items.body:
**Flexure formula:** $\sigma = Mc/I$, where $c$ is the distance from the neutral axis to the extreme fiber

**Rectangular section:** $I = bh^3/12$, $c = h/2$ (centroidal/symmetric section)

**Finding the critical section:** locate the maximum $|M|$ from the shear and moment diagrams first; it occurs where the shear diagram crosses zero ($dM/dx = V$)

**Unsymmetric sections:** check stress on both the tension and compression sides separately — $c$ may differ on each side.
